frame_analyses.description: Static view of FLS trainer with instruments positioned, no active manipulation visible
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean setup with instruments in neutral position

frame_analyses.description: Right needle driver grasping needle, positioning for suture placement
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle appears to be grasped at appropriate position

frame_analyses.description: Needle being driven through what appears to be Penrose drain material
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle entry angle appears perpendicular to tissue

frame_analyses.description: Both needle drivers working to complete suture passage through drain
Beginning knot formation with suture material, appears to be forming first throw
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Suture being manipulated for knot tying

frame_analyses.description: Continuing first throw formation with both instruments active
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Instruments working in coordination for knot formation

frame_analyses.description: Formation of second throw, suture configuration changing
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Appears to be proper hand switching between throws

frame_analyses.description: Final throw being completed, knot appears to be tightening
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good tension control visible during knot completion

frame_analyses.description: Scissors cutting suture tails, completing the task
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Both suture ends being cut within the trainer

penalties.description: Unable to clearly identify marked suture placement points due to camera angle and resolution

knot_assessments.notes: First throw appears to be double throw as required
Final throw with appropriate hand switching

confidence_rationale: Video quality and camera angle limit precise assessment of suture placement relative to marks. Knot formation and completion clearly visible, but exact deviation measurements cannot be determined reliably.
cannot_determine: Exact suture placement deviation from marks - marks not clearly visible in video
Precise gap assessment in drain slit - resolution insufficient for fine detail
technique_summary: Solid intermediate-level performance with proper knot sequence (double throw followed by two single throws with hand switching). Completion time of 168 seconds places this in intermediate range. All major technical requirements appear to be met.
strengths: Proper surgeon's knot sequence with double first throw
improvement_suggestions: Work on reducing overall time - current 168s vs expert benchmark of 80-120s
Focus on more efficient needle loading and suture placement phases
